name: aligned_input_changes
reason: Aligning an already-aligned value must be a no-op; changing it would corrupt valid base addresses

    |       -------------------------------------------------- at the end of the function body

522 |         align_to_usize(a, b) != a,

name: result_not_multiple
reason: The whole point of alignment is that the result is divisible by the alignment size; violating this breaks page-boundary invariants
output_tail:
ltiple(a: usize, b: usize)
    |       ------------------------------------------------ at the end of the function body
...
521 |         align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
    |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ failed this postcondition

name: excessive_rounding
reason: Rounding down by b or more means skipping an entire alignment block, losing a valid mapping region
output_tail:
ounding(a: usize, b: usize)
    |       ----------------------------------------------- at the end of the function body
...
522 |         a - align_to_usize(a, b) >= b,
    |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ failed this postcondition

name: not_idempotent
reason: Alignment must be idempotent; a double-align changing the result would mean walk result vbase addresses are unstable
output_tail:
